name: OIDC config block, construction guards & JWKS endpoint
outcome: OIDC can be turned on via an explicit config block, mistakes are caught at construction, and the server publishes its signing key at a JWKS endpoint.
scope: Add the oidc config block to AuthorizationServerOptions, fail-fast construction guards, and the jwks() endpoint method. No ID tokens, UserInfo, or discovery yet.
acceptance_criteria: Constructing with oidc config but no issuer throws a clear error
Constructing with oidc config and a symmetric JWT service (no getKeySet) throws a clear error
jwks() returns { keys: [...] } containing the public signing key
Constructing without oidc config behaves identically to today
steps: AuthorizationServerOptions — add an optional oidc config block (endpoint URLs, getUserClaims, optional getIdTokenClaims, optional metadata override)
AuthorizationServer constructor — throw when oidc config is present but issuer is unset
AuthorizationServer constructor — throw when oidc config is present but the JWT service does not expose getKeySet
AuthorizationServer — add a jwks() method returning the JWK set as a ResponseInterface
index.ts — export the new OIDC option types
test/unit — cover both construction guards and the jwks() output shape
notes: The oidc block lives on AuthorizationServerOptions so both AuthCodeGrant and the AuthorizationServer endpoint methods can read it. issuer is reused from existing options and becomes mandatory under OIDC. See docs/adr/0002.
